title: identity — field classification
description: Identity is the most privacy-sensitive aggregate in the system. Every credential column is `regulated_secret` (never returned on wire, never logged, never emitted to metrics). PII (email, dob) is `restricted` and gated behind the platform-admin `identity.view.pii` permission. Redactor rules always apply to every field of the Identity row unless the caller has that permission. See compliance.json for COPPA + GDPR floors.

regulated_minor: Identity.dob when dob resolves the caller to a minor under the applicable compliance floor (COPPA age_threshold OR GDPR per_country.minor_age)

wire_projection.description: The Identity row NEVER round-trips through a tenant-facing API. Every read is scoped to auth-service actions returning derived shapes (LoginResult, MfaChallengeResult, RegistrationResult). Platform-admin surfaces receive a redacted IdentityAdminData DTO.
wire_projection.tenant_wire_shape: N/A — no tenant endpoint returns Identity fields directly.

platform_admin_wire_shape.email_verified: boolean derived from email_verified_at IS NOT NULL
platform_admin_wire_shape.mfa_enabled: boolean derived from mfa_secret_encrypted IS NOT NULL
platform_admin_wire_shape.dob: date (REDACTED to year-only unless caller has `identity.view.pii`)

never_returned: Identity.mfa_secret_encrypted (regulated_secret — platform-admin surfaces show existence via `mfa_enabled` boolean, never the value)
Identity.email_verification_token_hash (regulated_secret — issued via one-shot email, never retrievable)

shape.dob: date (validated: bcp47_locale-aware date, compliance floor check)
shape.region_country_code: string (ISO-3166-2 alpha-2 — resolves the applicable compliance floor)

input_dtos.compliance_gate: Before creation, CompliantAgePolicy resolves the effective floor (US COPPA → 13; EU country → per_country.minor_age 13–16) and refuses if dob resolves the applicant to a minor below that floor.

shape.totp_secret: string (base32; server-generated on preview endpoint, echoed back to confirm)
shape.totp_confirmation_code: string (6-digit code the user typed from their authenticator)

output_dtos.purpose: Platform-admin listing + detail surface. See wire_projection.platform_admin_wire_shape above.

shape.totp_secret: string (base32 — shown once, then discarded; user re-submits to confirm)
shape.recovery_codes: string[] (10 codes × 10 characters; shown once at confirm)

notes: Every field on Identity is REDACTED by default at the audit layer. Redactor rules replace email with hash-fingerprint, dob with year-only, password_hash / mfa_secret_encrypted / mfa_recovery_codes_hashed with `[REDACTED]`. The `identity.view.pii` permission (platform_admin guard) unmutes email + dob; the regulated_secret set is NEVER unmuted.

The GDPR erasure hold-period (default 30 days) is a period during which Identity is soft-deleted; the row keeps `email` value + hashed credentials so a compliance-audit query for '  what did user X do' can still resolve. After the hold period, PurgeSoftDeletedIdentitiesJob hard-deletes the row and cascades to inbound FKs.

Minor identity handling: `dob` classification promotes from `restricted` to `regulated_minor` when the resolved age is below the applicable compliance floor. This is a compile-time classification, not a run-time check — the redactor sees the age-resolution outcome via the CompliantAgePolicy service.
Email is CITEXT in Postgres so `SELECT ... WHERE email = 'Alice@Example.COM'` matches the row stored as `alice@example.com`. The UNIQUE index is case-insensitive by construction.
